rationale: Title X is more informative for JICE because it states the comparator, study design, sample size, and the key 1-year outcome directly. It is slightly long and dense, but the result signal makes it stronger for readers scanning electrophysiology clinical studies.
loser_weakness: Title Y is clearer and more conventional, but it withholds the main efficacy result and uses broader wording such as “multicenter experience,” making it less specific and less compelling overall.
